paper_title: Materials and Methods of Fossil Specimen Collection and Analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting fossil specimens over a period of twenty years, involving systematic excavations by researchers, students, and collaborators.

steps.description: Almost 3300 fossil specimens have been collected in Milia as a result of investigations that lasted over twenty years (1996 to the present day).
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil specimens from Milia site

steps.description: A total of 40 participants (researchers, students and collaborators) participated in seventeen systematic excavations.

methods.description: Management of fossil specimen data using a digital database, including data entry, updates, and analysis using pivot tables and diagrams.

steps.description: Upon discovery, one of us (ET) entered each specimen into a digital database in Microsoft Access.
steps.searchable_summary: enter fossil data into Microsoft Access database

steps.description: The database also included historical (date of collection; collector), anatomical and taxonomic data, updated regularly following the detailed description and study of the various groups over the recent years.
steps.searchable_summary: update database with historical and taxonomic data

steps.description: This database formed the basis of the analyses herein by the calculation of the Pivot Tables in Microsoft Access and Excel.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pivot tables in Access and Excel

steps.description: Preliminary diagrams were created in Microsoft Excel and PAST 3.03, and then further edited in Adobe Illustrator for final presentation purposes.
steps.searchable_summary: create and edit diagrams for presentation

methods.description: Exclusion of certain database records that are not relevant to the study to ensure accurate analysis.

steps.description: For the analyses herein, the following database records have been excluded as not relevant to this study: two well preserved rhino specimens (a mandible and ulna) from the Priporos site and 30 fossilized wood specimens from MIL 1, 5, 6, and broader MIL sites.

methods.description: Calculation of rarefaction curves and estimation of minimum number of individuals and beta diversity for ecological analysis.

steps.description: Rarefaction curves are calculated following Lyman and references therein.
The minimum number of individuals is estimated based on Badgley, utilizing a range between the Number of Identified Specimens (NISP) and the Minimum Number of Individuals (MNI) counting techniques.
Beta diversity is based on Koleff et al. and references therein.
